comment_text.sn7.17:1.2: Navakammika, “builder”. The causative form is used, kārāpeti. He was not a manual laborer, but someone who supervised others to get the work done.
comment_text.sn7.17:3.2: Accept the reading visukkha, which is supported by SA2 96, 一切盡枯.
comment_text.sn7.17:3.3: This verse plays on vana (“jungle”) and vanatha, explained by the commentary as “undergrowth”, and applied in the sense of the tangles and “snarls” of craving.

root_text.sn7.17:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn7.17:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena navakammikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe kammantaṁ kārāpeti.
root_text.sn7.17:1.3: Addasā kho navakammikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ aññatarasmiṁ sālarukkhamūle nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn7.17:1.5: “ahaṁ kho imasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe kammantaṁ kārāpento ramāmi.
root_text.sn7.17:1.6: Ayaṁ samaṇo gotamo kiṁ kārāpento ramatī”ti?
root_text.sn7.17:1.7: Atha kho navakammikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami. upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn7.17:4.1: Evaṁ vutte, navakammikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.17:4.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn7.17:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn7.17:1.2: Now at that time the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Builder was having some building work done in that jungle thicket.
translation_text.sn7.17:1.3: He saw the Buddha sitting down cross-legged at the root of a certain sal tree, his body set straight, and mindfulness established in his presence.
translation_text.sn7.17:1.4: Seeing this, it occurred to him,
translation_text.sn7.17:1.5: “I enjoy having this building work done here in the jungle.
translation_text.sn7.17:1.6: I wonder what the ascetic Gotama enjoys having done?”
translation_text.sn7.17:1.7: Then Bhāradvāja the Builder went up to the Buddha and addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn7.17:2.1: “What kind of works are done by you,
translation_text.sn7.17:2.2: a mendicant in the sal jungle?
translation_text.sn7.17:3.1: “There’s nothing I need do in the jungle;
translation_text.sn7.17:3.2: my jungle’s cut at the root, it’s withered away.
translation_text.sn7.17:3.3: Free of snarls and thorns in the jungle,
translation_text.sn7.17:3.4: I enjoy solitude, having given up discontent.”
translation_text.sn7.17:4.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Builder said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.17:4.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

reference_text.sn7.17:1.1: csp1ed12.182, csp2ed12.182, dr15.249, ms12S1_1271, msdiv203, pts-vp-pli1ed1.180, pts-vp-pli2ed1.387, sya15.264
